completed.notes: Cube + ground proof of life landed before swarm started.
Landed pre-orchestrator. Three camera presets (SideCam, AngledCam ~30°, TopCam) + BaseLighting + drei <Sky> + 1/2/3 keyboard switcher. Files under src/renderer/game3d/{cameras,lighting,scenes}/.

Stood up Rapier. PhysicsWorld + usePhysicsDebug ('P' key). ProofOfLife ground became fixed RigidBody w/ explicit CuboidCollider; one dynamic smoke-test cube falls from y=3. Camera switcher 1/2/3 untouched. Reviewer flagged one minor note: usePhysicsDebug doesn't guard keydown autorepeat — non-blocking.

Manifest schema (permissive, additionalProperties:true) + useScreenAssets() hook (drei useGLTF.preload/clear, returns {models, ready}) + sample proof-of-life manifest + public/game3d-assets/{models,textures,audio,manifests}/ scaffold. KTX2 deferred to cycle 2 (drei v9.122 export confirmed clean). Reviewer flagged one LOW note: hooks-in-map pattern in useScreenAssets.js:76 — worker documents the key-prop remount constraint, drei's own useGLTF carries the same constraint. Two prior reviewer dispatches stalled before writing receipt; a third with model=sonnet succeeded — see .claude/swarm/blockers/reviewer-no-receipt-2026-04-25T08-05-00Z.md.

Took two cycles. Cycle 1 (NEEDS_REVISION): landed useInput()/InputProvider/InputDebugHUD/TouchJoystick + InputProvider wrap of Game3D, but gamepad action/cancel was not edge-triggered, rAF polled getGamepads() unconditionally, and TouchJoystick.jsx had a dead var. Cycle 2 (PASS): rising-edge guard via prevGpButtons ref; connectedRef gates the getGamepads() call via gamepadconnected/disconnected events; dead var removed. CRITICAL checks (scope, camera-switcher 1/2/3 intact, physics-debug 'P' intact, KEY_MAP avoids reserved keys, no TS, no package.json) clean across both cycles.

run_history.scope: state.json + 8 receipts + sequencing.yaml + asset-manifest schema + sample manifest
state.json + 16 receipts (was 8) + sequencing.yaml + asset-manifest unchanged. Both new tts-* .md files confirmed.

completed.notes: Always-on validator. Two runs this session, both ALL PASS. Run 1 covered the Engine Foundation pod outputs (8 receipts). Run 2 re-validated after the Audio & TTS pod landed (16 receipts total). Both runs initially stalled on haiku model and recovered with sonnet override per saved memory.

6 NPCs discovered (zuzu/mrs_ramirez/mr_chen/old_miner/desert_guide/river_biologist). CHARACTER_GENDER expanded with one-line comments per entry; zuzu→default per user requirement, all five others gender-inferred from honorifics+given names with no ambiguity. _pickVoiceByGender('default') returns null. speak() got an explicit gender==='default' branch that leaves utterance.voice unset (bypasses the natural-English fallback heuristic). Module-level _voiceCache primed on load + voiceschanged listener (with onvoiceschanged fallback) re-fills it; all synth.getVoices() reads now go through _getCachedVoices(); only direct call sits in the cache-refill helper. getVoiceForCharacter and speakAsNpc default unknown ids to 'default' (was 'male'). voicePreference backfilled on old_miner (0.85/0.9), desert_guide (0.9/1.0), river_biologist (0.95/1.05); pre-existing mrs_ramirez/mr_chen untouched. debugListVoiceAssignments() exported. Public signatures of speak/speakAsNpc/autoSpeak preserved. Note: dispatched via general-purpose agent shell because the new agent type wasn't in this session's Agent registry (registry loads at session start; new .md files mid-session aren't picked up). Standards from .claude/agents/tts-voice-config.md were embedded in the prompt and reviewer confirmed PASS.

Took two cycles. Cycle 1 (NEEDS_REVISION on 2nd reviewer pass): resolveSpeakerToNpcId helper at GameContainer.jsx:47-69 + dialog-TTS effect rewrite at lines 509-531 + speaker→npcId fallback fix + Option N: choice-text append matching the physics-question pattern. Worker correctly left the physics path alone; the first reviewer pass FAIL on a 'physics path scope violation' was a misattribution of a pre-existing user edit (GameContainer.jsx hadn't been committed since e2531ce, so git diff HEAD mixed the user's earlier physics-TTS edit with the worker's actual changes — re-review with corrected baseline retracted the FAIL). The remaining HIGH was that HONORIFIC_RE stripped Mr/Mrs/Ms/Dr but the actual CHARACTER_GENDER keys keep those prefixes. Cycle 2 (PASS): single-line regex change /^(mr|mrs|ms|dr|ranger)\.?\s+/i → /^(ranger)\.?\s+/i. Now Mrs. Ramirez/Mr. Chen resolve to mrs_ramirez/mr_chen and hit the gender map. Routed via general-purpose for both cycles since the new tts-dialog-integration agent type isn't in this session's Agent registry.

First World & Discovery pod agent. 9 regions discovered + tagged: arizona/arabian → desert, andes/pakistan → mountain, turkish → grassland, persian → rock, swahili → water, chinese/malay → unknown (enum has no multi-biome or jungle/forest values). BIOME frozen enum + getBiome(regionId) helper added; existing exports (REGIONS default, REGION_MAP, getRegionMaterial, getAllMineableMaterials, RARITY_CHANCE) preserved. Notable judgment call (PASS confirmed): every region already had a non-canonical `biome:` field with descriptive strings ('desert_scrub', 'temperate_steppe', etc.); worker normalized values in-place to the canonical enum rather than introduce a parallel field. Reviewer's independent grep confirmed no consumer reads `region.biome` from region data — all `.biome` references in src/ are runtime `state.environment.biome` paths. Zero behavioral risk. Reviewer also caught a latent bug in the spec: spec example used REGIONS[regionId]?.biome (REGIONS is an array, would always return undefined); worker correctly used REGION_MAP[regionId]?.biome. MEDIUM follow-up: questGenerator.js:210 and MCPSystem.js:221 still use stale `'desert_scrub'` fallback string — pre-existing, not a regression, flagged for a future migration agent. Routed via general-purpose since the new agent type isn't in this session's Agent registry; reviewer was native (sonnet override per saved memory).

Parallel dispatch with world-terrain-renderer. Created NEW src/renderer/game/systems/discoverySystem.js (sparse Set<string> storage keyed by `${gx}_${gy}`, DISCOVERY_TILE_SIZE=32) with the full 7-export public API (initDiscovery, revealArea, isDiscovered, getDiscoveryState, loadDiscoveryState, resetDiscovery, DISCOVERY_TILE_SIZE) + 1 debug helper (_debugGetRevealedSet). Wired into saveSystem.js additively without bumping schema version (CURRENT_VERSION still 4) — the existing `{ ...defaultSave(), ...migrated }` pattern handles missing `discovery` field on old saves. defaultSave gains `discovery: { tiles: [], width: 0, height: 0, tile: 32 }`; loadGame calls loadDiscoveryState in try/catch; saveGame pulls live state via getDiscoveryState. Worker verified backwards-compat with a node integration test (old v4 save without discovery field hydrates to empty Set). Memory budget: 1024×768 fully discovered = ~5.6KB JSON. Reviewer PASS, all CRITICAL clean. MEDIUM follow-up (non-blocking): inner try/catch around getDiscoveryState() in saveGame swallows failures silently — should add console.warn for dev visibility. Routed via general-purpose; reviewer native sonnet override.

completed.human_gate_approval: User said 'go phaser-hmr-bridge' after orchestrator presented the staged plan. Lean fix (window.__phaserGame in dev) was already applied in GameContainer.jsx prior to dispatch.
completed.notes: Closes the Vite-HMR-vs-Phaser-state gap. New helper src/renderer/game/dev/phaserHmr.js exports registerSceneHmr(sceneKey, hot, sceneClass, exportName='default') — production-safe early return on falsy hot, reads window.__phaserGame, swaps via game.scene.remove + game.scene.add(KEY, NewClass, true) inside try/catch with [phaserHmr] info logs. Wired 15 concrete scenes (the full ALL_SCENES from config.js): OverworldScene, ZuzuGarageScene, StreetBlockScene, DogParkScene, LakeEdgeScene, SportsFieldsScene, CommunityPoolScene, DesertTrailScene, MountainScene, WorldMapScene, DesertForagingScene, CopperMineScene, SaltRiverScene, GarageScene (legacy), NeighborhoodScene (legacy). Two abstract bases skipped: LocalSceneBase.js, BaseSubScene.js — confirmed by grep against config.js ALL_SCENES and game.scene.add calls. Each scene file got exactly two additive lines (top import + bottom registration); reviewer confirmed no other edits attributable to this worker (parallel session's terrain/discovery edits in WorldMapScene/CopperMineScene/etc. correctly attributed to baseline). Static checks: npm run build SUCCESS in 10.58s with HMR block tree-shaken; npm run lint shows 259 pre-existing repo errors but zero new from this patch. Reviewer LOW (non-blocking): could not locally re-verify production tree-shaking since dist/ not present at review time. Routed via general-purpose; reviewer also via general-purpose.

First parallel-batch agent of the post-terrain World & Discovery push. Worker stalled at max_turns running a post-edit `acorn`/`node --check` syntax validation; code edits were fully on disk. Orchestrator ran RECEIPT_RECOVERY (verify → synthesize → audit log) per user-approved policy 2026-04-25 — verification: file exists, all expected symbols present (hash2 import line 20, _renderPathLayer line 310, hash2 usage line 393, lineBetween block deleted), node --check returns SYNTAX_OK, sibling landmarks's receipt independently confirmed merge with no collision. Recovered receipt covers all 7 spec items: bezier paths via 20-step quadratic approximation, biome-keyed colors (DESERT 0xa08250/0.6/2px, GRASSLAND 0x6e5a32/0.65/2px, MOUNTAIN 0x3c3228/0.75/3px, default 0x786446/0.6/2px), depth -50 between terrain and nodes, WATER endpoints skipped (TODO ferry), deterministic curve sign via hash2(0x9a7b, sumCharCodes(a.id), sumCharCodes('neighborhood')), locked paths darkened to 35% alpha. Spec item 5 (dashed pending paths) deferred — TODO at lines 306-308 because data/discoveryUnlocks.js does not exist yet (will be authored by world-discovery-quests). Reviewer PASS — all CRITICAL clean, all HIGH clean, MEDIUM/LOW non-blocking (no perf.now bracket; world-map-polish can add). Reviewer explicitly noted 'all synthesized receipt fields match observed code' — recovery was accurate.

Second parallel-batch agent. Worker completed cleanly — wrote its own receipt and even cross-confirmed path-renderer's parallel merge in its notes ('Path-renderer agent edits were fully merged before landmark layer was added. No collision occurred.'). Implements: _renderLandmarkLayer (~194 lines) with deterministic SEED=0x5eed (matches terrain heightmap), XOR sub-seed 0xABCD1234 to avoid aliasing terrain detail-pass rolls, jittered tile placement, biome-gated spawn probabilities (desert 8%/grassland 4%/mountain 6%/rock 5%/urban 2%/water 0%/unknown 0%), LANDMARK_CAP=80 enforced on both loop conditions, all 5 procedural shape inventories per spec (desert 70/30 cactus/rock-formation, grassland scrub, mountain ridge lines, rock boulder via Graphics.slice, urban structure outline + door hint), 1px ellipse shadow at rgba(0,0,0,0.15) per landmark, performance.now() bracket + console.debug log with full per-biome breakdown. Estimated count for current 2-region map: ~44 (desert 42 + mountain 2). GRASSLAND/ROCK/URBAN counts will rise once worldMapData.js gains regions in those biomes. Reviewer PASS first time on cycle 1 (after re-dispatch with depth-question pre-resolved — first reviewer run stalled mid-investigation). LOW non-blocking note: _landmarkContainer has no explicit setDepth (defaults to 0, same as nodes); creation-order keeps nodes on top today but a defensive setDepth(-25) is recommended for world-map-polish. LOW: bounds-check guard at line 693 is logically redundant dead code (harmless).

v2 REWRITE — replaced v1's radial fillCircle blobs with continuous tile-based terrain. Implementation: 32 px tiles (~28×21=~588 tiles on 900×615 parchment) iterated via nested for-loops, single batched Graphics object using fillRect per tile. v2 strong-identity palette (DESERT 0xE6C27A, GRASSLAND 0x7FBF6A, WATER 0x3A7BA8, MOUNTAIN 0x7E6855, ROCK 0x5C5048, URBAN 0x9C886A, UNKNOWN 0x3C3530). Inter-region blending via 1/d² weighted blend of 3 nearest region anchors per tile → smooth biome transitions, no hard borders. Coarse fbm heightmap (HM_DIVISOR=5, ~6×5 cells) modulates tile brightness 0.85+h*0.30 with +0.3*weight uphill bias for MOUNTAIN-anchored tiles. Per-biome detail pass adds desert sand-grain stipple, grassland green patches, water faint horizontal bands, rock dots, urban grid hint — all gated by dominant biome and routed through deterministic rand2(seed,ix,iy). NEW src/renderer/game/utils/terrainNoise.js (65 LOC, 0 deps) exports hash2/rand2/valueNoise2/fbm2/generateHeightmap/sampleHeightmap. v1 deletion confirmed: 3-stack fillCircle loop and 12-seed desert stipple loop fully removed; reviewer verified no fallback/toggle preserved. Pre-existing phaser-hmr-bridge 4-line block (line 19 import + bottom registration at lines 542-543) preserved verbatim. Performance estimate: 3-6ms inside the 16ms budget; console.debug logs actual measured ms on every load (kept intentionally as dev signal). LOW observations (non-blocking): grassland/water/urban/rock detail passes are latent — only fire when world-biome-classifier registers additional regions in worldMapData (currently only arizona_desert=DESERT and mountain_range=MOUNTAIN); console.debug retained could be removed in polish. Routed via general-purpose; reviewer native sonnet override.

superseded.superseded_reason: v1 produced concentric tan fillCircle blobs at WorldMapScene.js:240-280 — visually wrong (dead-looking radial gradient centered on each node). User rewrote spec to v2 (continuous tile/noise terrain + biome blending + elevation shading) in commit 328ca93. v2 must DELETE the v1 radial blob code wholesale, not toggle.
superseded.notes: Parallel dispatch with world-discovery-state. Added terrain rendering layer to WorldMapScene.js in three isolated zones: lines 13-46 (BIOME import + BIOME_COLORS warm palette + WORLD_REGION_BIOME bridge map + biomeForWorldRegion helper), lines 75-86 (call to _renderTerrainLayer in create() after parchment rect), lines 219-289 (the _renderTerrainLayer method itself — single _terrainContainer at depth -100, 3 concentric translucent fillCircles per location for soft-edged blobs ~225px radius on 1024px map, deterministic stipple dots on desert blobs, geometry-mask clipped to parchment). Spec palette matched exactly. Render-once at create(). Click handlers (setInteractive/pointerdown/pointerover/pointerout) byte-identical. Notable judgment call (PASS confirmed): worldMapData.js REGIONS use different ids (arizona_desert, mountain_range) than the cross-cultural regions.js (arizona, andes); worker added an inline WORLD_REGION_BIOME bridge map inside WorldMapScene rather than touch worldMapData.js (which is data-layer scope owned by biome-classifier). Pre-existing dirty-baseline audio block at lines 212-216 (transitionToScene swap) preserved verbatim per dispatch warning. First reviewer dispatch stalled mid-task before writing receipt — re-dispatched fresh and second run completed cleanly with PASS. LOW observations (non-blocking): all 3 current locations are in arizona_desert so DESERT blobs heavily overlap (intentional — reads as one Sonoran landscape); home/neighborhood node has no terrain blob underneath (floats on parchment); future tuning when non-Sonoran locations land. Routed via general-purpose; reviewer native sonnet override.
